name: Search existing consignor or exporter
pageTitle: Search for an existing consignor or exporter
caption: Traders
heading: Search for an existing consignor or exporter
secondaryActions: Create a new consignor or exporter
layout: govuk-grid-row > govuk-grid-column-full (search form) + govuk-grid-column-one-third (Create link); results table full width

structure.text: Search for an existing consignor or exporter
Columns: Name | Address | Country | View | Select. Each row has a View button and a Select button (custom link-button).
Create a new consignor or exporter

govukComponents.usage: Page H1 'Search for an existing consignor or exporter'
Groups the three search inputs (Name, Address, Country) under the legend 'Search'
Name and Address free-text search fields
Field labels Name / Address / Country (plain govuk-label, no size modifier)
Search results table (Name, Address, Country, View, Select columns)
'Create a new consignor or exporter' link (id=add-economic-operator) -> /traders/consignor/new
govukComponents.evidence: trace eddc6f6ec852d692c454d319ade1607727a84a4f action 42; corroborated as a link (ARIA role=link) by QA page object SearchExistingConsignorOrExporterPage.ts:6-8 — this is the ONLY control the automation drives on this page, and every CHED workflow + the accessibility test clicks it (page-objects/notification/SearchExistingConsignorOrExporterPage.ts:6, workflows/notification/ched-d-workflows.ts:257, tests/accessibility/ched-d-accessibility-tests.spec.ts:91)

govukComponents.usage: Visible horizontal rule separating page regions

nonStandardPatterns.pattern: button.link-button (View and Select buttons in every results row)
nonStandardPatterns.whatItDoes: Renders each row's 'View' and 'Select' actions as <button> elements styled to look like links inside table cells.
nonStandardPatterns.concern: Bespoke class, not a Design System component. Two actions per row is a common IPAFFS results-table pattern. In the rebuild these should be govuk links or a single Design System action per row; a full table of link-buttons is a custom widget.
nonStandardPatterns.govukAlternative: govuk-link (or a Button used as the row action) — GOV.UK has no 'link-button' component

nonStandardPatterns.pattern: nav.pagination with pagination-list / pagination-item / pagination-item-next / pagination-link / pagination-link-icon / pagination-link-label / pagination-link-title
nonStandardPatterns.whatItDoes: Custom pagination control showing 'Next page : 2 of 3258' with a next-page link/icon.
nonStandardPatterns.concern: Entirely bespoke pagination markup rather than the GOV.UK Pagination component. Rendered '3258' pages of seed data is a fixture artefact, but the paginator itself is non-standard. Also note only a 'Next page' control is present — no Previous / numbered pages observed.

nonStandardPatterns.whatItDoes: IPAFFS-specific layout wrappers for the search form and the results rows (name/address cell markup).
nonStandardPatterns.concern: Bespoke layout/semantic classes (and a bare non-prefixed 'form-group' alongside govuk-form-group). Presentational only, but they signal hand-rolled page structure rather than standard grid + form-group composition.

nonStandardPatterns.whatItDoes: Responsive results-table wrapper that renders separate desktop and mobile table variants (the 'transporter-table-mobile' class is reused here for the traders results).
nonStandardPatterns.concern: Custom responsive-table implementation with duplicated desktop/mobile DOM, not the standard GOV.UK approach (a single table in a scrollable container). Doubles the markup and risks divergence between the two variants.
nonStandardPatterns.govukAlternative: A single govuk-table inside an overflow-x scroll container; no separate mobile DOM
nonStandardPatterns.evidence: trace eddc6f6ec852d692c454d319ade1607727a84a4f action 42 (class inventory: table-responsive, traders-search-results-form-desktop/mobile, transporter-table-mobile)

fields.name: name
fields.label: Name
fields.evidence: trace eddc6f6ec852d692c454d319ade1607727a84a4f action 42 (INPUT|text|name|name|false). QA corroboration: NOT required — no test in the corpus ever fills this input. Every CHED-D/P/PP workflow and the ched-d accessibility test bypasses the search entirely by clicking 'Create a new consignor or exporter' (workflows/notification/ched-d-workflows.ts:256-257; tests/accessibility/ched-d-accessibility-tests.spec.ts:89-91). The page object exposes no locator for this field (page-objects/notification/SearchExistingConsignorOrExporterPage.ts). Search-with-terms behaviour is therefore a gap in the QA repo too, not just the traces.
fields.requiredEvidence: legacy — confirmed optional. The consignor search Joi schema declares name as Joi.string().max(255).allow('') with no .required(), i.e. a free-text search filter, not a mandatory field (ipaffs-frontend-notification/service/src/validation/routes/handlers/importer/traders/search.js:20-22). Corroborates the rendered 'not required' observation. This is a SEARCH FILTER — the PartOne model mandatoriness for consignor data-capture lives on the create/edit pages, not on this search page.
fields.conditionalRender: Rendered only when 'name' is in economicOperatorSettings[type].inputs. For consignor that set is [name, address, country], so it always renders on this page (ipaffs-frontend-notification/service/src/utils/economic_operator.js:37-40; template gate views/importer/traders/search.html:26-36).

fields.name: address
fields.label: Address
fields.evidence: trace eddc6f6ec852d692c454d319ade1607727a84a4f action 42 (INPUT|text|address|address|false). QA corroboration: NOT required and never driven — no page-object locator exists and no workflow/test fills it (page-objects/notification/SearchExistingConsignorOrExporterPage.ts; workflows/notification/ched-d-workflows.ts:256-257).
fields.requiredEvidence: legacy — confirmed optional. Joi schema declares address as Joi.string().max(255).allow('') with no .required() (validation/routes/handlers/importer/traders/search.js:23-25). Search filter, not a mandatory field.
fields.conditionalRender: Rendered only when 'address' is in economicOperatorSettings[type].inputs; present for consignor (economic_operator.js:37-40; template views/importer/traders/search.html:37-47).

fields.name: country
fields.label: Country
fields.evidence: trace eddc6f6ec852d692c454d319ade1607727a84a4f action 42 (SELECT|country; document.querySelectorAll('#country option').length === 254 — full country list is reference data, 253 countries/territories plus the 'Select country' placeholder; note UK is represented as separate England / Northern Ireland / Scotland / Wales options, not 'United Kingdom'). QA corroboration of the UK-as-four-nations modelling: types/country.ts:238-241 defines England=GB-ENG, NorthernIreland=GB-NIR, Scotland=GB-SCT, Wales=GB-WLS as distinct entries (no 'United Kingdom' key), and lists crown dependencies separately (Guernsey=GG:95, Jersey=JE:116). The search <select> itself is never driven by any test (no page-object locator), so this is inferred structural corroboration only — not an exercised selection.
fields.requiredEvidence: legacy — confirmed optional. Joi schema declares country as Joi.string().valid(...countries).allow('') with no .required(); the empty '' placeholder is the default and a valid submission (validation/routes/handlers/importer/traders/search.js:35-37). The submitted value must be a known reference-data ISO code, else it is rejected with 'Country is not valid'.
fields.conditionalRender: The country dropdown is gated by BOTH showCountryDropDown AND 'country' being in economicOperatorSettings[type].inputs (template views/importer/traders/search.html:73-83). showCountryDropDown = !isReimport(notification) (route handler routes/handlers/importer/traders/search.js:187), so on a re-import journey the country filter is hidden and the search is forced to the UK (payload.country = UK_ISO_CODE, search.js:94-97). For a normal (non-reimport) consignor search it renders. The options are sourced from the countries + UK-regions reference-data services, not a static list (search.js:155-158; template partial data/countriesSelectHighLow).

fields.name: crumb / etag / returnUrl / fromFooterHeader
fields.label: (hidden anti-forgery + state fields)
fields.evidence: trace eddc6f6ec852d692c454d319ade1607727a84a4f action 42 (INPUT|hidden crumb, etag, returnUrl, fromFooterHeader — framework/CSRF, not user data)

validationMessages.message: Name must be 255 characters or fewer
validationMessages.evidence: ipaffs-frontend-notification/service/src/validation/routes/handlers/importer/traders/search.js:20-22 (Joi.string().max(255), STRING_MAX -> maxLengthMessage(labels.NAME, 255)); template maxLengthMessage('Name must be {n} characters or fewer') at validation/messages/en.js:3-5; label 'Name' at validation/labels/en.js:67. Never rendered in any trace or QA test — the search inputs are never exercised — so this is a legacy-derived message, not a confirmed one.

validationMessages.message: Address must be 255 characters or fewer
validationMessages.evidence: validation/routes/handlers/importer/traders/search.js:23-25 (Joi.string().max(255), STRING_MAX -> maxLengthMessage(labels.ADDRESS, 255)); validation/messages/en.js:3-5; label 'Address' at validation/labels/en.js:3.

validationMessages.trigger: Submitted country value is not one of the reference-data ISO codes (e.g. a tampered/stale query param). The empty '' placeholder is allowed and does NOT trigger this.
validationMessages.evidence: validation/routes/handlers/importer/traders/search.js:35-37 (Joi.string().valid(...countries), ANY_VALID -> invalidMessage(labels.COUNTRY)); invalidMessage(field) => `${field} is not valid` at validation/messages/en.js:19-21; label 'Country' at validation/labels/en.js:34. The valid set is built at request time from the countries + UK-regions reference-data services (search.js:161).

validationMessages.trigger: Non-numeric ?page query param on the results pagination (hidden framework param, not a user-facing form field)
validationMessages.evidence: validation/routes/handlers/importer/traders/search.js:44-46 (Joi.number(), NUMBER_TYPE -> typeMessage(labels.PAGE)). NOTE a legacy DEFECT: typeMessage(label, type) => `${label} must be a ${type}` is called with only one argument (validation/messages/en.js:27-29), so the message literally renders 'Page must be a undefined'. Recorded verbatim as the legacy source produces it; the rebuild should not reproduce this — the pagination page param does not need a user-facing type-error message at all.

validationNotes: Legacy validation on this page is EXCLUSIVELY max-length (name/address, 255) and valid-value (country against reference data) checks on optional search filters, plus a numeric check on the hidden ?page param. There is NO required-field validation anywhere — every filter allows '' and has no .required(). This corroborates the rendered/QA observation that no error state was ever exercised: an all-blank search is valid and simply returns the unfiltered list. Field mandatoriness for consignor DATA (PartOne model) belongs to the create/edit-consignor pages, not to this search page.
openQuestions: No validation was observed on this page. A search with all fields blank may simply return the full list (results shown even before any search term); confirm whether any field is ever required — likely gap, no error state exercised in the mostly-passing corpus.

The Country <select> holds 254 options (253 countries/territories + 'Select country' placeholder), verified by document.querySelectorAll('#country option').length === 254. Only the first 17 are captured verbatim; the full list is reference data the new app should source from a reference-data service. Note the UK appears as four separate options (England, Northern Ireland, Scotland, Wales) rather than a single 'United Kingdom'.
Each results row exposes both a 'View' and a 'Select' action as custom link-buttons; the rebuild should decide whether both are needed or whether row selection can be a single Design System action.

Pagination is bespoke and only a 'Next page' control was observed (label 'Next page : 2 of 3258'); no Previous or numbered-page controls seen — confirm the full paginator surface. The '3258' page count is a seed-data artefact.

Results-table data in traces is Lorem-ipsum seed fixtures (Astra Rosales / Linus George Ltd, Switzerland) — treat row content as fixtures, not requirements. The column set (Name, Address, Country, View, Select) is the real requirement.

This search/list page is a precursor step to 'Create a new consignor or exporter'; the evidence actions both click that Create link. The search itself (typing a term + pressing Search) was not exercised in these two traces, so no observedValues were captured — the field structure is confirmed but search behaviour/results filtering is a gap.

CORROBORATION (both sources agree, gap NOT closable): the QA automation repo confirms the search flow is never exercised. The page object exposes a single locator — the 'Create a new consignor or exporter' link (page-objects/notification/SearchExistingConsignorOrExporterPage.ts:6-8) — and every CHED workflow (ched-d/p/p) plus the accessibility test click straight through it (workflows/notification/ched-d-workflows.ts:256-257; tests/accessibility/ched-d-accessibility-tests.spec.ts:89-91). So the Name/Address/Country search inputs, the results-table View/Select actions, and the pagination have NO test coverage and NO trace of being used. Their DOM is confirmed (rendered); their behaviour (search filtering, selecting an existing operator, paging) is a genuine gap for a human — the new app should decide whether an 'existing operator' search is even needed, or whether create-new is the only real path.

The sibling search pages follow the identical minimal pattern — SearchExistingConsigneePage.ts and SearchExistingTransporterPage.ts each expose only their 'Create a new ...' link and are also always clicked through. This suggests the consignor/consignee/transporter/economic-operator search-then-select surface is a single reusable IPAFFS pattern that the rebuild could model once.

No validation message or error assertion for this page exists anywhere in the QA repo (grep of tests/ finds only accessibility checks and create-link clicks). The traces and QA never rendered any validation, but the legacy source DOES define it — see validationMessages (legacy): max-length on name/address, valid-value on country, and a numeric check on the hidden ?page param. None are required-field validations.

LEGACY BRANCH (reimport): on a re-import consignor journey the Country dropdown is suppressed (showCountryDropDown = !isReimport) and the search is forced to the UK (payload.country = UK_ISO_CODE), and the 'Create new' link points at the CONSIGNEE new-operator URL rather than consignor (routes/handlers/importer/traders/search.js:70-72, 94-97, 187). Not observed in the CHED-D traces (which are non-reimport). The rebuild should decide whether the reimport variant of consignor search is in scope.

LEGACY: this /traders/{type}/search template is fully shared across all economic-operator types; the visible inputs differ per type via economicOperatorSettings[type].inputs (consignor = name+address+country; some types add Type/Approval Number/Post Code). Reinforces the earlier note that consignor/consignee/transporter search is one reusable pattern (economic_operator.js:37-79).
